notes: Reproduced: Linear gradient shader DrawRect shows a ~27x per-iteration slowdown in SkiaSharp v3.x vs v2.88.9 (4.6ms/iter vs 0.17ms/iter at 1920x1080). The gradient/solid ratio went from 1.1x in v2.88.9 to ~39x in v3.x. The regression persists on latest stable (3.119.2) and main branch. The C# and C API wrappers are trivially thin — the performance difference is in the upstream Skia raster gradient pipeline which changed between the Skia milestones used in SkiaSharp v2 (m88) and v3.

reproductionSteps.description: Create console project with SkiaSharp 3.116.0 (reporter's version)
reproductionSteps.command: mkdir -p /tmp/skiasharp/repro/3109 && cd /tmp/skiasharp/repro/3109 && dotnet new console -n Repro --framework net8.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 3.116.0

filesCreated.description: Benchmark comparing DrawRect with gradient shader vs solid paint over 500 iterations at 1920x1080

reproductionSteps.description: Run gradient benchmark with SkiaSharp 3.116.0 — gradient is ~39x slower than solid fill
reproductionSteps.output:
SkiaSharp version: 3.116.0.0
Gradient DrawRect: 500 iterations in 2281.1ms (4.56ms/iter)
Solid DrawRect:    500 iterations in 58.8ms (0.12ms/iter)
Gradient/Solid ratio: 38.8x
Gradient per-frame overhead: 4.44ms/iter
RESULT: Significant performance degradation with gradient shader
SUCCESS: test completed, output.png saved

reproductionSteps.description: Run same benchmark with SkiaSharp 2.88.9 (reporter's last-good version) — gradient only 1.1x solid
reproductionSteps.output:
SkiaSharp version: 2.88.0.0
Gradient DrawRect: 500 iterations in 84.7ms (0.17ms/iter)
Solid DrawRect:    500 iterations in 78.1ms (0.16ms/iter)
Gradient/Solid ratio: 1.1x
Gradient per-frame overhead: 0.01ms/iter
RESULT: Gradient shader performance is acceptable
SUCCESS: test completed, output.png saved

reproductionSteps.description: Run benchmark with latest stable SkiaSharp 3.119.2 — still ~39x gradient/solid ratio
reproductionSteps.output:
SkiaSharp version: 3.119.0.0
Gradient DrawRect: 500 iterations in 2310.8ms (4.62ms/iter)
Solid DrawRect:    500 iterations in 59.5ms (0.12ms/iter)
Gradient/Solid ratio: 38.8x
Gradient per-frame overhead: 4.50ms/iter
RESULT: Significant performance degradation with gradient shader
SUCCESS: test completed, output.png saved

reproductionSteps.description: Run benchmark with main branch source — still ~39x gradient/solid ratio
reproductionSteps.output:
SkiaSharp version: 3.119.0.0
Gradient DrawRect: 500 iterations in 2297.7ms (4.60ms/iter)
Solid DrawRect:    500 iterations in 58.5ms (0.12ms/iter)
Gradient/Solid ratio: 39.3x
Gradient per-frame overhead: 4.48ms/iter
RESULT: Significant performance degradation with gradient shader
SUCCESS: test completed

versionResults.notes: Gradient performance acceptable — 0.17ms/iter, ratio 1.1x vs solid fill
Gradient 4.56ms/iter, ratio 38.8x — significant regression from v2.88.9
Gradient 4.62ms/iter, ratio 38.8x — still present on latest stable
Gradient 4.60ms/iter, ratio 39.3x — still present on main branch

actionability.reason: Clear performance regression confirmed across all v3 versions including main. The ~27x per-iteration slowdown for gradient shader rendering is in the upstream Skia raster pipeline, not the SkiaSharp C# wrapper. Requires profiling the Skia gradient fill path to identify the bottleneck.

actions.description: Add tenet/performance label to reflect confirmed performance regression
Confirm reproduction with benchmark data and suggest bitmap-caching workaround
Cross-reference to broader v3 performance regression report #3525

workarounds: Pre-render the gradient to an SKImage once (when size changes) and draw the cached image each frame instead of applying the shader per-frame
Use GPU-accelerated rendering (SKGLView instead of SKCanvasView) which handles gradient shaders natively
missingInfo: Whether the reporter is using SKCanvasView (CPU raster) or SKGLView (GPU)
Approximate dimensions of the gradient-filled rectangle
How frequently PaintSurface is invoked (animation loop, timer, user-triggered)
